model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Duke enters this Final Four matchup as the nation's top-ranked team, boasting the #1 adjusted defensive efficiency and a top-5 offense that provides an elite two-way balance. While Florida is the defending national champion and possesses a top-5 defense of its own, the Blue Devils' superior overall efficiency and their 67-66 head-to-head victory over the Gators in December give them the edge. Despite navigating the tournament without starting guard Caleb Foster and center Patrick Ngongba II, Duke's interior length and disciplined half-court execution have remained dominant, making them the favorite to advance to the title game.

reasoningStep.title: Final Four Analysis: Duke vs. Florida
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed the matchup between #1 Duke and #1 Florida, focusing on analytical profiles, head-to-head results, and injury impacts.
evidence: Duke holds the #1 overall ranking in KenPom and Torvik, featuring the nation's top adjusted defensive efficiency.
Florida is the defending national champion and ranks in the top 10 for both offensive and defensive efficiency.
Duke won the only regular-season meeting between these teams, a 67-66 victory on December 2, 2025.
Despite injuries to Caleb Foster and Patrick Ngongba II, Duke's depth and interior presence have led them through a difficult East region.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: f4-1
Current game label: National semifinal 1
Round: Final Four
Resolved teams: Duke (duke) vs Florida (florida)
Prior committed picks: 64
